inputs.label: standard
inputs.original: The district's teacher turnover rate was 24% in 2025
inputs.article: Low pay, high turnover: the districts losing their teachers
inputs.where: Third paragraph and in the caption of the accompanying bar chart
inputs.how: The analyst inadvertently used the 2024 figure instead of 2025
inputs.affects_conclusion: No — the corrected figure (18%) still makes this the highest-turnover district in the region

inputs.label: minimal
inputs.original: The city spent $14.2 million on road repairs

inputs.label: european
inputs.original: Hungary's press freedom score declined by 12.4 points between 2020 and 2025
inputs.article: Press freedom in the EU: the 2025 scorecard
inputs.where: Fourth paragraph and in the data table row for Hungary
inputs.how: The 2020 score used in the calculation was for a different index year (2018) due to a mislabeled column in the source spreadsheet
inputs.affects_conclusion: Partially — Hungary remains the lowest-scoring EU member state, but the magnitude of decline is smaller than originally reported. The article's characterization of Hungary's trajectory as 'the steepest decline in the EU' needs to be verified against other countries' actual declines.
